blog_posts.title: Stumbling Through Market Conversations in Our New Language
blog_posts.excerpt: A simple trip to the vegetable market turned into a crash course in humility and vocabulary.
blog_posts.content:
Today I braved the local market alone with my notebook, phrase list, and a lot of nervous energy. My goal was simple: buy tomatoes, onions, and greens in the local language without switching back to English. I had practiced the phrases all morning, but the moment the vendor smiled and fired back a question, my mind went blank.

Instead of pretending I understood, I tried one of the first phrases we learned: “Please speak slowly, I am learning your language.” His face lit up. He repeated himself slowly, threw in some gestures, and even corrected my pronunciation as we went. Several other vendors joined in, laughing kindly when I mixed up words for “onion” and “banana.”

Walking home, arms full of produce and scribbled new vocabulary, I realized these awkward conversations are becoming holy moments. Each stumble is a step toward deeper connection, and every correction is an act of hospitality from our neighbors. Language learning is slower than we hoped, but God is using even the market to teach us patience, humility, and joy.

blog_posts.title: The Day a Wrong Tone Changed My Sentence Completely
blog_posts.excerpt: In tonal languages, a small shift in tone can mean a very big difference in meaning.
blog_posts.content:
Our teacher warned us that tones matter more than we realize. I nodded along politely until I tried to tell a neighbor, “Your baby is very cute,” and accidentally said something much closer to “Your baby is very expensive.” Thankfully, she burst out laughing and then spent the next five minutes patiently drilling the correct tone with me.

Moments like this used to leave me wanting to hide at home with my textbook, but now we are learning to laugh at ourselves and receive correction as a gift. When we choose to keep speaking, we communicate something deeper than perfect grammar: that we value people enough to risk embarrassment.

If you think of us, pray that we would stay bold and humble in this language learning journey—that every mistake would become an opportunity for relationship instead of a reason to retreat.

blog_posts.title: Why We’re Learning to Listen Before We Speak
blog_posts.excerpt: Before sharing the gospel in a new language, we’re learning the posture of listening.
blog_posts.content:
In our first months here, I imagined language school would be about mastering verbs, memorizing vocabulary, and finally being able to tell our testimonies clearly. All of that is happening, but something deeper is going on too. Our teachers keep reminding us that language is more than words—it is worldview, humor, politeness, and pain.

So we’re practicing listening. In class, we listen to recordings of everyday conversations, then sit with local friends and ask simple open-ended questions. We resist the urge to rush to our own stories, and instead we ask, “How did that feel?” or “What do you think God was doing in that moment?”

This slower path feels counterintuitive when we long to share good news, yet we sense the Lord inviting us to patience. Pray that as we learn to listen well in this new language, the Spirit would guide us into the right words at the right time.

blog_posts.title: Surviving Our First Week of Language School
blog_posts.excerpt: We finished our first week of language school with tired brains and full hearts.
blog_posts.content:
We thought we knew tired before, but language school is a new kind of exhaustion. Every sound feels unfamiliar, every word is slippery, and our mouths simply don’t want to cooperate. But in the middle of all the tongue twisters and grammar drills, God keeps giving us small encouragements: a phrase understood on the bus, a neighbor who smiles when we greet them correctly, a child who giggles and repeats our new words.

There are days we wonder if we’ll ever preach or teach in this language, but right now faithfulness looks like showing up, doing our homework, and trusting that God will multiply our small efforts. Thank you for praying us through this beginning stage. We feel carried.

blog_posts.title: Our First Month in Kenya: Small Wins and Honest Tears
blog_posts.excerpt: From power outages to unexpected kindness, here’s a glimpse into our first month on the field.
blog_posts.content: One month in, Kenya already feels both wonderfully familiar and completely overwhelming. The kids have learned the names of the guards at our gate, we’ve survived our first bout of stomach bugs, and we’re getting used to planning around frequent power cuts. In the middle of the chaos, God keeps surprising us with kindness.

blog_posts.title: Finding Homeschool Rhythms on the Field
blog_posts.excerpt: Homeschooling between language classes and ministry visits is stretching us in new ways.
blog_posts.content: Balancing homeschool with ministry and language study has pushed us to simplify. We’re learning that faithfulness in this season looks like focusing on the essentials and letting go of our picture-perfect school days.

blog_posts.title: Field Report: Mobile Clinic Outreach in the Hills
blog_posts.excerpt: Over three days our team partnered with a local hospital to serve remote villages.
blog_posts.content: We just returned from a three-day mobile clinic in the hills, partnering with a nearby hospital and local church leaders. We saw long lines of patients, prayed with families, and watched God open doors for deeper relationships in these communities.

blog_posts.title: Visa Approved! Next Steps Toward the Field
blog_posts.excerpt: After months of uncertainty, our visas have finally been approved.
blog_posts.content: Today we received the email we’ve been waiting for: our visas are approved. This opens the door for us to move forward with tickets, packing, and goodbyes.

blog_posts.title: Lessons from 24 Hours of Travel with Kids
blog_posts.excerpt: Our first intercontinental flight as a family taught us flexibility, creativity, and dependence on grace.
blog_posts.content: From midnight layovers to lost stuffed animals, our long-haul travel to the field stretched all of us. Yet we saw God’s grace in small mercies along the way.

blog_posts.title: Church Plant Update: First Baptisms in the River
blog_posts.excerpt: This month we celebrated the first baptisms from the new fellowship.
blog_posts.content: Standing by the river as new believers shared their testimonies, we were reminded again that God is building His church in ways far beyond our strength.

blog_posts.title: Reimagining Holiday Traditions Far from Home
blog_posts.excerpt: We’re learning to celebrate Advent in ways that make sense in our new culture.
blog_posts.content: This year we traded snow for sunshine and sweaters for short sleeves, but the heart of our holiday traditions remains the same: remembering Jesus’ coming.

blog_posts.title: Mid-Year Financial Update and Gratitude
blog_posts.excerpt: An honest look at our current support level and where we see God providing.
blog_posts.content: We want to steward your generosity with transparency. Here’s where our support stands mid-year and how we are planning for the coming season.

events.title: Houston Missions Evening - Stories from the Field
events.description: An evening gathering in Houston, Texas to hear stories from the field, pray together, and learn how to partner in ongoing projects. Light refreshments will be served. Evening and afternoon session options are available; please choose your preferred time when you RSVP.

events.title: Dallas Partner Dessert - Vision for the Next Season
events.description: Join us in Dallas, Texas for an evening dessert reception where we will share vision, photos, and specific ways to pray for the coming year.

events.title: Austin Youth Gathering - Living on Mission
events.description: A youth-focused evening in Austin, Texas exploring what it means to live on mission locally and globally.

events.title: Denver Missions Brunch
events.description: A Saturday morning brunch in Denver to share updates from Kenya and Peru and invite new prayer partners.

events.title: Online Prayer Night for Asia Hospital Ministry
events.description: A one-hour online prayer gathering focused on hospital chaplaincy and medical outreaches across Asia.

events.title: San Diego Missions Sunday
events.description: Sharing testimonies and teaching during the morning services in San Diego.

events.title: Nairobi Pastor Training Livestream
events.description: An online livestream from Nairobi, Kenya of a one-day training for rural pastors.

events.title: Chicago Small Group Q&A Night
events.description: An informal Q&A and prayer time with a small group in Chicago.

gift_items.title: School Supplies Pack for One Student
gift_items.description: Provide notebooks, pencils, and basic supplies so one student can fully participate in class for a term.

gift_items.title: Children’s Storybook Bible in Local Language
gift_items.description: Help place a beautifully illustrated storybook Bible into the hands of a child in their heart language.

gift_items.title: Language Learning Flashcards for Local Believers
gift_items.description: Support local believers serving as translators and evangelists with durable language flashcards.

gift_items.title: Pastor’s Training Manual Set
gift_items.description: Provide a set of contextualized training manuals for one rural pastor.

gift_items.title: Basic Medicine Kit for Rural Clinic Day
gift_items.description: Equip a mobile clinic day with basic medicines for treating common illnesses.

gift_items.title: Solar Lantern for One Family
gift_items.description: Provide a durable solar lantern so a family without reliable electricity can study Scripture at night.

gift_items.title: One Week of Bible School for a Student
gift_items.description: Sponsor one week of room, board, and tuition for a Bible school student from a remote area.

gift_items.title: Emergency Food Bundle for a Family
gift_items.description: Provide a week’s worth of staple foods to a family experiencing crisis.

gift_items.title: Motorbike for Village Outreach
gift_items.description: Help purchase a motorbike so local leaders can reach remote villages with the gospel.

gift_items.title: Community Water Filter System
gift_items.description: Provide a large-capacity water filter system for a community meeting space or church.

countries.name: Kenya
countries.overview: Kenya is a diverse nation in East Africa with bustling cities and remote rural communities. The family partners with local churches to invest in leadership development, community health, and discipleship among youth.
countries.prayer_needs_summary: Pray for rural pastors with limited access to training, for the growth of new fellowships, and for wisdom as the family navigates cross-cultural ministry and language learning.

countries.name: Peru
countries.overview: Peru’s coastal cities, Andean highlands, and jungle regions each present unique ministry opportunities. The family partners with local believers to strengthen discipleship and outreach in under-resourced areas.
countries.prayer_needs_summary: Pray for effective discipleship of new believers, healthy partnerships with Peruvian church leaders, and open doors in communities that have been resistant to the gospel.

countries.name: Thailand
countries.overview: Thailand is a majority-Buddhist nation where many have never heard a clear presentation of the gospel. The family serves short-term alongside local ministries focused on university students and marginalized communities.
countries.prayer_needs_summary: Pray for language acquisition, boldness and sensitivity in sharing Christ, and deep friendships with Thai believers and seekers.

countries.name: United States
countries.overview: The United States serves as the family’s sending base, where they regularly return to share updates, raise support, and encourage churches toward global mission.
countries.prayer_needs_summary: Pray for rest and renewal during home assignments, clear communication of vision, and new partners to join in prayer and financial support.

prayer_updates.title: Hospital Outreach in Kathmandu: Doors Opening in the Ward
prayer_updates.summary: Our team has begun regular visits to a government hospital in Kathmandu, Nepal.
prayer_updates.content:
Twice a week, a small team now visits the general ward of a busy government hospital in Kathmandu. The hallways are crowded and the needs feel overwhelming. Yet the head nurse has welcomed us to sit with families, listen, and pray when asked. Last week, one mother who had been sleeping on the floor beside her son’s bed asked, through tears, if we would pray for peace in the middle of their fear.

Please pray for strength and protection for the team as they walk the hospital corridors, for sensitivity to the Holy Spirit, and for lasting relationships with the staff. Ask God to turn this hospital into a place where many encounter His comfort and hope.

prayer_updates.title: Praying for Children in the Bangkok Hospital Ward
prayer_updates.summary: Our partners in Bangkok have been invited into the children’s ward of a major hospital.
prayer_updates.content:
In Bangkok, our local church partners have received permission to visit families in the children’s ward of a large city hospital. Many of the parents have traveled long distances and are sleeping on benches beside their children’s beds. The atmosphere is heavy with fear and exhaustion.

Please pray for physical healing for the children, encouragement for weary parents, and wisdom for the chaplain team as they offer prayer in the name of Jesus. Ask the Lord to protect them as they move from room to room in the hospital, and to open spiritual conversations that lead to lasting hope.

prayer_updates.title: Church Partnership with Rural Mission Hospital
prayer_updates.summary: A small church is partnering with a rural mission hospital to care for patients and families.
prayer_updates.content:
In a remote Asian town, a mission hospital has faithfully served for decades, but staff are weary and resources are thin. A nearby church has begun sending volunteers twice a week to sit with patients, read Scripture, and pray quietly in the wards.

Pray that this partnership would strengthen both the hospital staff and the local church. Ask God to refresh exhausted nurses and doctors, to provide funds for needed medicines, and to use the gentle presence of believers in the hospital to point many toward Jesus.

prayer_updates.title: Mobile Clinic Outreach in Rural Kenya
prayer_updates.summary: Our team is partnering with a local clinic to reach villages without regular medical care.
prayer_updates.content: This month we are traveling with a Kenyan medical team to host mobile clinic days in three remote villages. Please pray for stamina, safety on rough roads, and opportunities to share the hope of Christ as we serve practical needs.

prayer_updates.title: Training New Hospital Chaplains in Nairobi
prayer_updates.summary: A group of 15 believers is being trained to serve as volunteer chaplains.
prayer_updates.content: In Nairobi, we are launching a six-week training for believers who feel called to serve in local hospitals. Pray for clarity as we teach how to listen well, offer Scripture, and pray in sensitive contexts. Several public hospitals have already expressed openness to chaplain visits.

prayer_updates.title: Youth Retreat in the Peruvian Highlands
prayer_updates.summary: Dozens of teens are gathering for a three-day discipleship retreat.
prayer_updates.content: Please cover the youth retreat in prayer—many of these teens are the only believers in their families. Ask God to deepen their roots and call some into long-term ministry.

prayer_updates.title: Language Learning Breakthroughs and Fatigue
prayer_updates.summary: Our family is experiencing both progress and tiredness in language school.
prayer_updates.content: We are grateful for small victories in conversations with neighbors, but our brains often feel overloaded. Pray for renewed joy in language learning, good rest, and deep relationships with our teachers.

prayer_updates.title: Refugee Outreach in a European City
prayer_updates.summary: Local churches are opening their doors to refugees arriving from multiple nations.
prayer_updates.content: Pray for wisdom, unity, and resources as believers serve families who have lost homes and loved ones. Ask that many would encounter Christ’s love through practical care.

prayer_updates.title: Strength for Our Sending Churches
prayer_updates.summary: Several of our sending churches are walking through leadership transitions.
prayer_updates.content: Please pray for wisdom and unity for pastors and elders, and for continued passion for global missions even in the midst of local challenges.

prayer_updates.title: Discreet Bible Distribution in the Middle East
prayer_updates.summary: Believers are quietly sharing Scripture in a sensitive context.
prayer_updates.content: Pray for protection, discernment, and open hearts as a small team distributes Bibles and digital Scripture resources where access is restricted.

prayer_updates.title: Drought Relief Efforts in East Africa
prayer_updates.summary: Families are facing crop failure and food shortages due to prolonged drought.
prayer_updates.content: Ask God to send rain, provide wisdom for local leaders, and multiply limited resources as churches distribute food and water in Jesus’ name.

prayer_updates.title: Family Health and Rest
prayer_updates.summary: Several family members have been fighting recurring illnesses.
prayer_updates.content: Please pray for protection over our health, wisdom in balancing ministry and rest, and grace for our kids as they adapt to new routines.

projects.title: Education Scholarships for Village Students
projects.summary: Provide school fees and supplies so vulnerable students in rural Kenya can stay in the classroom.
projects.description: Many families in rural Kenya cannot afford uniforms, books, and school fees. This project helps cover these costs for students who would otherwise drop out. Scholarships are administered through local churches that know the families personally. Gifts support both immediate school needs and long-term mentoring relationships.

projects.title: Village Reading Corners
projects.summary: Set up simple reading spaces with storybooks and Bibles in local languages.
projects.description: In partnership with local churches, we are creating small reading corners in village churches and community centers. Each corner will include shelves, mats, children’s storybooks, and Bibles in the local language. Your one-time gift helps launch new spaces where kids can encounter both literacy and the gospel.

projects.title: Village Kids Meal Support
projects.summary: Provide simple, nutritious meals for children who attend weekly Bible clubs.
projects.description: For many children in this rural community, the snack at our weekly Bible club is the most substantial food they receive that day. This project funds simple but nutritious meals—rice, beans, and fruit—served through local churches. Monthly partners keep the pots full and the doors open.

projects.title: Language Tutors Stipend Fund
projects.summary: Support local language tutors who help our family and new missionaries learn well.
projects.description: Skilled local tutors are essential to deep language and cultural learning. This fund provides fair wages for tutors who invest hours each week helping us understand grammar, idioms, and cultural nuances. As we grow in fluency, we can share Christ more clearly.

projects.title: Rural Evangelism Travel Fund
projects.summary: Help cover fuel and transport costs so local leaders can reach remote villages.
projects.description: Reaching remote villages often requires hours of travel over rough roads. This fund helps cover fuel, vehicle maintenance, and simple lodging for outreach teams as they visit unreached communities with local partners.

projects.title: Hospital Chaplain Visits in Asia
projects.summary: Resource chaplain teams as they visit patients and families in crowded hospitals.
projects.description: Chaplain teams in several Asian cities are entering busy hospital wards with Scripture portions, simple gifts, and a listening ear. Monthly support provides transportation, training materials, and follow-up resources for families who request ongoing visits.

projects.title: Community Ministry Center Rent
projects.summary: Keep the doors open at a small ministry center used for Bible studies and outreach.
projects.description: This simple storefront space hosts language classes, Bible studies, and youth gatherings throughout the week. Monthly gifts cover rent and utilities so the center can remain a stable, welcoming place in the neighborhood.

projects.title: One-Time Launch of Radio Bible Program
projects.summary: Help fund production equipment and initial airtime for a new radio Bible teaching program.
projects.description: Local pastors have been invited to host a weekly Bible teaching program on community radio. A one-time campaign will cover microphones, basic studio gear, and the first six months of airtime. After that, local churches will sustain the program.

projects.title: Bible Translation Support for Local Team
projects.summary: Provide monthly support for a local translation team working on Scripture portions.
projects.description: A small team of local believers is translating key passages of Scripture into a minority language that has very few resources. Monthly support helps cover stipends, reference materials, and field testing trips.

projects.title: Pastors’ Library Starter Sets
projects.summary: Equip rural pastors with a basic library of biblical and theological resources.
projects.description: Many rural pastors have only a worn Bible and few other resources. This project provides a small set of carefully chosen books in their language, along with simple study tools to help them teach faithfully.

projects.title: Youth Camp Sponsorships
projects.summary: Send teens from remote communities to a week-long discipleship camp.
projects.description: A week at youth camp can be a turning point for young believers. Your gift covers transportation, food, lodging, and program costs for teens who could not otherwise attend.

projects.title: Field Vehicle Purchase
projects.summary: Purchase a reliable vehicle for field visits, outreaches, and airport runs.
projects.description: Our current vehicle is nearing the end of its life and is increasingly unreliable on long trips. This project will help purchase a used 4x4 vehicle capable of handling rough roads while carrying our family and team members safely.

stories.title: A Night of Healing in the Village
stories.excerpt: What began as a simple prayer meeting turned into a night the village will not forget.
stories.content:
The small church building was packed, with people standing outside the open windows to listen. We had planned only to share a short message and pray for a few sick members. But as we began to sing, neighbors started bringing those who were ill—an elderly woman with chronic pain, a child with a high fever, a man whose leg had been injured in a motorbike accident.

We felt our weakness and lack of expertise. Yet as we prayed in Jesus’ name, we saw God move in ways we could not explain. The elderly woman reported her pain had vanished. The child’s fever broke and he began to sit up and drink water. The injured man walked without crutches for the first time in weeks. The room filled with worship and tears.

We left that night humbled, reminded that miracles are not about our faith being strong enough, but about God’s compassion for people. Please keep praying that these physical healings would lead to deeper spiritual transformation in the village.

stories.title: Provision at the Border Crossing
stories.excerpt: With our documents in question and funds running low, we saw God provide in a surprising way.
stories.content:
We had followed every instruction we knew, but the border officer shook his head and handed our passports back. Our paperwork was missing a small stamp from an office we did not even know existed. The nearest town was hours away and our funds were almost gone.

As we sat on our bags and prayed, a pastor from a nearby village happened to walk by. Recognizing our local language textbooks, he struck up a conversation. Within an hour he had called a friend who worked in the necessary office, arranged transportation, and even insisted on covering the unexpected fee. By evening, we were back at the border with the correct stamp and were waved through without trouble.

It was a quiet miracle—no bright lights or dramatic music, just the precise help we needed at the exact moment we needed it. God reminded us again that He goes before us, even through bureaucracy.

stories.title: A Child’s Fever Broke After Simple Prayer
stories.excerpt: In a crowded hospital ward, one mother asked for prayer, and God answered tenderly.
stories.content:
The pediatric ward was overflowing, with children sharing beds and mothers sleeping on the floor. Our team moved quietly between families, asking if anyone wanted prayer. One mother clutched her little boy, whose body was hot with fever that had not responded to medicine.

With her permission, we laid a gentle hand on the child’s arm and prayed a simple prayer in Jesus’ name. There were no dramatic sensations, no visions—just a quiet trust that God cared for this family. When we returned later that afternoon, the mother rushed toward us smiling. The fever had broken, and her son was sitting up asking for food.

Doctors and nurses continued their vital care, and we do not claim to understand how God weaves together medicine and miracle. But we left with a fresh sense of His nearness in that hospital ward. Please pray that many more families there would encounter His comfort and power.

stories.title: Storm Stilled on the Lake
stories.excerpt: Caught in a sudden storm on the lake, we saw God answer a desperate prayer.
stories.content:
Our boat was small and the storm rolled in faster than we expected. Waves slapped against the sides and the motor sputtered. With no life jackets and no nearby shore, fear rose quickly among the team. One local leader began to sing a simple worship chorus, his voice barely audible over the wind, and we joined in as best we could.

As we sang and prayed, the wind slowly began to die down and the rain eased. Within minutes the lake was calm enough for the driver to restart the motor and guide us safely to shore. Later, he admitted that he had been certain we would capsize.

We know storms do not always stop so quickly, but that day we sensed the Lord reminding us that He holds both the lake and our lives in His hands.

stories.title: A Dream That Led to Safety
stories.excerpt: God used a dream to reroute our plans and protect a local believer.
stories.content:
One of our closest local partners woke unsettled from a vivid dream in which a road was blocked and soldiers were searching vehicles. As he prayed, he sensed the Spirit warning him not to travel his usual route that day. Instead, he chose a longer, more difficult road.

Later we learned that there had indeed been a checkpoint and unrest on his normal route, and several people were detained. We do not know exactly what would have happened if he had gone that way, but we are grateful for God’s gracious warning and our brother’s obedience.

stories.title: The First Time I Shared My Testimony in the Local Language
stories.excerpt: After months of study and many mistakes, the gospel finally flowed in a new tongue.
stories.content: I had practiced my testimony dozens of times with my language tutor, but sharing it with a neighbor felt terrifying. My words came slowly, but God used them to open a beautiful conversation.

stories.title: Adjusting to a New Culture as a Family
stories.excerpt: From food to friendships, each family member is navigating change differently.
stories.content: Our move to the field has revealed both our weaknesses and God’s faithfulness. Each member of the family is processing transition in unique ways, and we are learning to listen and support one another.

stories.title: When a Support Gap Was Quietly Filled
stories.excerpt: Facing an unexpected financial shortfall, we watched God stir hearts in three different places.
stories.content: A sudden change in exchange rates and increased rent left us with a significant gap in our monthly budget. We committed to pray before sending out any urgent email. Within two weeks, three separate friends contacted us saying they felt prompted by God to begin or increase their giving.

stories.title: Youth Camp Testimony from the Peruvian Highlands
stories.excerpt: At camp, a quiet teen from a remote village encountered God’s love in a new way.
stories.content: During small group time, a usually quiet teen opened up about the pressure he feels as the only believer in his family. Surrounded by peers and leaders who listened and prayed, he left camp with renewed courage.

stories.title: The Small Daily Miracles We Almost Missed
stories.excerpt: Not every miracle is dramatic—many look like strength for another ordinary day.
stories.content: We often think of miracles as spectacular events, but lately we are seeing God’s hand in small, daily ways: a timely word of encouragement, an unexpected friend for a lonely child, stamina for one more language class.

country_team_members.bio: James and Maria serve as team leaders in Kenya, focusing on pastor training, mobile clinics, and mentoring young leaders in rural churches.
Elijah pastors a growing church in the highlands and coordinates village outreach trips with the missionary team.
Grace is a registered nurse who helps lead mobile clinic days and trains church volunteers in basic health education.
Peter works with teens and young adults, leading Bible studies, youth camps, and discipleship groups in several villages.
Ana helps coordinate small group discipleship in the Peruvian highlands and mentors young women in their walk with Christ.
Carlos leads youth retreats and outreach events, partnering with local churches to equip teens as disciples and witnesses.
Somchai pastors a small congregation near a university campus and partners with the team in student outreach.
Rachel coaches new workers in language learning strategies and helps them understand Thai cultural norms.
Miriam hosts English conversation clubs and small groups for university students exploring faith and life questions.
David serves as lead pastor of one of the family’s primary sending churches and helps coordinate care during home assignments.
Emily manages communication with prayer and financial partners, helping share stories and organize events when the family is in the US.

event_sessions.name: Afternoon Session
event_sessions.description: Afternoon session with field stories and Q&A, ideal for families with younger children.

event_sessions.name: Evening Session
event_sessions.description: Full evening presentation with extended prayer time and project highlights.

event_sessions.name: Evening Dessert Session
event_sessions.description: Single evening dessert reception with vision sharing and partner prayer.

event_sessions.name: Youth Evening Session
event_sessions.description: Interactive youth-focused session with teaching, discussion, and response time.

event_sessions.name: Missions Brunch Gathering
event_sessions.description: Single brunch session including meal, presentation, and prayer for Kenya and Peru.

event_sessions.name: Online Prayer Session
event_sessions.description: One-hour Zoom call focused on hospital ministry prayer needs across Asia.

event_sessions.name: Morning Service Session
event_sessions.description: Primary morning service with missions sermon and brief field update.

event_sessions.name: Full Training Day Livestream
event_sessions.description: All-day online access to pastor training sessions from Nairobi.

event_sessions.name: Small Group Evening Session
event_sessions.description: Informal living-room style Q&A and prayer time with a small group in Chicago.
